content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 4,
1993
Frederick A.
Gage
Ventura County Alliance of Taxpayers
4435 McGrath Street, Suite 304
Ventura,
CA
93004
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No.
A—93-289
Dear Mr.
Gage:
This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice provided to you concerning the propriety of using
campaign funds under the “personal use” provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
This letter is based on facts
provided during our telephone conversation on July 20,
1993,
and
in your letter dated July 23,
1993.
QUESTION
May a ballot measure committee which is established to oppose
a county tax use campaign funds to make a donation to the Ventura
County Alliance of Taxpayers,
a nonprofit organization set up to
work in the anti-tax field?
CONCLUSION
The ballot measure committee may use campaign funds to make a
donation to the nonprofit organization if all of the following
apply:
(1)
the organization is
a, bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, religious,
or similar tax—exempt nonprofit
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000
.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
Sections 89510-
89522,
which describe the permissible use of campaign funds,
are
referred to as the “personal use” laws.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A—93-289
Page2
organization;
(2)
no substantial part of the proceeds of the
donation will have a material financial effect on the campaign
treasurer,
or any individual or individuals with authority to
approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by the committee,
or member of his or her immediate family;
and
(3)
the donation of
campaign funds bears a reasonable relation to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.
FACTS
The Ventura County Alliance, of Taxpayers is a nonprofit
organization organized to work in the anti-tax field.
The
organization intends to establish a local ballot measure committee
to oppose a county tax which is to be voted upon in November 1993.
ANALYSIS
The general rule of the personal use provisions of the Act is
that an expenditure of campaign funds
2 by a committee not
controlled by a candidate must be, at a minimum, reasonably
related to a political, legislative,
or governmental purpose.
Where an expenditure of campaign funds confers a substantial
personal benefit on any individual or individuals with authority
to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by the
committee, the expenditure must be directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
(Seótion
89512.5.)
In other sections, the personal use law provides express
standards for specific types of expenditures.
For example,
expenditures for donations from campaign funds are governed by
Section 89515.
Section 89515 provides:
*
Campaign funds may be used to make donations or
loans to bona fide charitable, educational, civic,
religious,
or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit
organizations, where no substantial part of the
proceeds will have a material financial effect on
the candidate, elected officer,
campaign treasurer,
or any individual or individuals with authority to
approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a
committee,
or member of his or her immediate family,
and where the donation or loan bears a reasonable
relation to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.
2
“Campaign funds” which may be donated include cash,
cash
equivalents,
and other assets of the committee.
(Section
89511(b) (1).)


Our File No.
A-93-289
Page
3
You ask regarding the propriety of using campaign funds of a
ballot measure committee, which you intend to establish, to make a
donation to a nonprofit organization which you also represent, the
Ventura County Alliance of Taxpayers.
Pursuant to Section 89515,
campaign funds held by a ballot measure committee may be used to
make donations to nonprofit organizations provided three
requirements are met.
First, the recipient organization must be a bona fide
charitable, educational, civic, religious,
or similar tax—exempt
nonprofit organization.
You have identified the Ventura County
Alliance of Taxpayers,
a nonprofit corporation established to work
on issues related to taxes,
as the organization which will receive
the donation.
It would appear that this organization qualifies
under Section 89515 as either a civic or similar tax-exempt
nonprofit organization.
The second requirement provides that no substantial part of
the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the campaign
treasurer,
or any individual or individuals with authority to
approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a committee,
or
member of his or her immediate family.
The Commission has advised
that the proceeds of campaign funds will have a “material
financial effect”
on any individual or individuals with authority
to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by the
committee,
members of their immediate family,
or the committee
treasurer if a payment received by such individual from a
nonprofit organization described in Section 89515 is $250 or more.
(Bhatia Advice Letter,
No.
1-92-017; Weiss Advice Letter,
No.
A-91-158).
For example, if a ballot measure committee’s
donation of campaign funds increases the income or assets of the
committee’s treasurer by $250 or more, the donation will likely be
deemed to have a material financial effect on the treasurer and be
prohibited.
(Spain Advice Letter,
No.
1-90-410.)
Finally, the donation or loan must bear a reasonable relation
to a political,
legislative,
or governmental purpose.
According
to our telephone conversation on July 20,
1993,
you wish to know
whether the ballot measure committee which you intend to establish
to oppose a county tax on the November ballot may make a donation
to the Ventura County Alliance of Taxpayers after the election is
over.
The donation would be made for the purpose of continuing
the nonprofit organization’s work in the anti-tax field which is
also the purpose for establishment of the ballot measure
committee.
Thus, it would appear that such expenditure would be
reasonably related to a political purpose which is a permissible
expenditure of campaign funds under Section 89515.
Thus, if all of the requirements of the statute are complied
with as discussed above,
the donation of funds would be
appropriate pursuant to Section 89515.


Our File No.
A—93—289
Page
4
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-5901.
Sincerely,
Wayne Ordos
Executive Director
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division

(3)
the donation of
campaign funds bears a reasonable relation to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.
